result.stdout:
Usage
	Read schema from file and writes to out file
		bare <schema-file> <out-file>
	Read schema from stdin and writes to stdout
		bare --stdin

Examples
	bare schema.bare Messages.cs
	echo "type Token data<16>" | bare --stdin | less
	echo "type UUID data<16>" | bare --stdin -c UUID System.Guid MyEncoding.encode_Guid MyEncoding.decode_Guid | less

Options
	--help
		Prints this help text.
	--stdin
		Reads schema from stdin and writes code to stdout.
	-l --lang <fs|cs>
		The target language to produce code for.
		default: cs
	-lt --list-type <list|array|readonly>
		Defines which underlying type the code generator should use for lists.
		default: array
	-ns --namespace <name>
		Types and encoding class will be in the given namespace.
		default: "Bare.Msg"
	-class --classname <name>
		Name of the encoding class
		default: "Encoding"
	-c --custom <name> <type> <encoder> <decoder>
		Inject a custom type in the generated code. Multiple custom types possible.
		name - type name declared in the schema file, which will be replaced.
		type - full name of the custom type
		encoder - full name of the encoder function
		decoder - full name of the decoder function
	-ws --whitespaces <count>
		Count of whitespaces used for indentation. When set the C# code will be indentated with whitespaces instead of tabs.
		default for F#: 2
	-sc --snake-case
		Converts type and field names to Snake_case
